Okay. Well, what we do is it plays forever. It plays forever. Here's the thing about it. You will not read any of that no matter how well it's done until you're in the market for furniture. And the people who are in the market for furniture are not a static group. They are moving for a—people come in and out to the market. And this is our attempt to catch the net-based. You got this? Okay. And if you do most of the same thing, it's this three or four or five times a year, everybody that lives in the area is going to give this mail from one of my clients. And not everybody—I mean, once every three or four years, they come in and market for a car. But because they have repeatedly gotten special offers from that dealership, when they are in the market, that's where we are. And they—a lot of them say, well, I waited until I got another piece of mail because I need that to check in. And that's something I'd like to ask you about on the clutter thing. You—I mean, whatever price you're going to offer, how do you feel about putting a 50 or 100 dollar check on the next 30 minutes? What we do a lot of times, whenever we do this recording, we always put a check. You have to cover the bonus distance and I'll cover the check. And we address the check and that's what shows the window on the outflow. It says pay to the owner of the top QF. Are you asking me? What was the question? How do you feel about putting a check or a discount on a high price? I feel pretty good about it. But I believe that I like to do it the way J.A. Bram talks about doing it, is that I'm buying it for you. We have—as a matter of fact, how do I feel about it? Let me tell you how strongly I feel that that's a good idea. Because—well, I'm looking for the SAM stuff again. That is the one that we did this month that brought in the most business we attached to check. I think I read that. Let's see if I can find the paragraph. Dear Mrs. So-and-so, I have enclosed my personal check made payable to you in the amount of $20. I have not yet signed this check. I like this. I have not yet signed this check, but I will be happy to do so next Thursday, April 4th, anytime between the hours of 10.45 a.m. and 9 p.m. Here is why. My name is Leslie Fishbind, and I am in trouble. I am the owner of KC Fine Furniture. We have five stores in Colorado, and I now have a problem that is very unusual. As you know, the East Coast has been periodically paralyzed by massive amounts of snow this winter. And it goes on and on through the story. And then it tells them that they will bring in the check. She will sign it, and they will have the $20. And that they do not have to buy anything. She is just bribing them to come in and look at the picture. Sam and I sat down and figured out how much it cost him to get someone to walk in the door. It is something like $60 to $80. They are just together for the first walk. So why not just pay them $20? Basically, this is going to a select audience and we are saying, hey, you look like you might want to buy some furniture. I will give you $20 to come in the store and look at this. And they do. They come in and look at it. And another thing we did is about you. I give the impression sometimes that I believe you should always use a close-based envelope. Always use direct impression addressing. Always use a live stamp. Always mail first-class mail. That is not entirely correct. I do believe that you have never done direct mail before, that that is the only thing you should do. Because it gives you the highest percentage chance of success and eliminates a lot of the reasons why mailings can go bad. However, you know, sometimes I meter my mail and send it off. Sometimes I put labels on it. And sometimes I fool around with this priority mail stuff, et cetera, et cetera. And I think sending a check, if you work it, if you work it very well, I do like that idea. Yep. I think about the sales letters on the audience. Actually, for me, that works very well. That works very well. I think I love the first person best and the opposite person best. But it's never really done before. I don't want to just use a cover letter with the cover. I mean, of course you should use a cover letter, but I don't really know where to stop from the coffee and go to the tables. Well, I'll tell you what I do. Sometimes at the end of the seminar, I will pass a mic around and ask people what they like the best about the seminar. I can't hear my own voice. I don't think I see that in my testimonials on audio or video. And then I'll send that tape out with the sales letter about the divine tapes at the seminar and they'll hear people right there saying, this is the seminar. You should get the tapes. And then that's kind of supportive. But let me tell you, which brings up, there's two things of particular interest that I want to talk about this morning that are not usually covered in advertising seminars and not covered in direct mail and they shouldn't. If you want to know how good a copywriter really is, ask him how good he is on segues. And most copywriters will not be very good at segues and most of them won't even know what they are. And I'm not going to embarrass any of you copywriters in the room to ask you what segues are. I don't even know how to spell it. I think it's S-C-G-U-E, it's right. And I'm going to do segues for you. And I'm going to show you how good I am at them. And by doing so, I will illuminate the importance of them. A segues is a transition from something that catches your attention to another area of interest. And the segues needs to make sense and it needs to be logical. Now I'm going to give you an example of a segues that you all know about so that you'll know what it is and then I'll do some off the cuff for you. You can use segues with grabbers or you can use them with copy or whatever but just to illustrate it, let's use it with a grabber. A grabber, let's say that we have a pin attached to the top of this letter. That's the attention to get it right. And the letter is, dear friend, as you can see I have attached a nice fresh copper pin to the top of this letter. Why have I done this? Actually there are two reasons. One, I have something very important to tell you. Two, and secondly, since what I have to talk to you about concerns making or saving money, I thought using the pin as a little eye capture was appropriate. Here's what it's all about. That was a segues. Now we're going to take this gentleman with the blood test here. And we're going to send letters with something attached to the top of the letter. It can be a paper clip, a penny, anything you want. You call out what it is and I will instantly give you the segues for it. Beach ball. Dear friend, actually you can't use a beach ball because you can't attach. Well we'll use a photo of a beach ball. Will that work? Okay. Dear friend, as you can see I have attached a photograph of a beach ball to the top of this letter. Why have I done this? The answer is simple. Last year I was playing volleyball in front of Larry O's restaurant on Ocean Drive with a couple of very attractive young ladies in bikinis. When I jumped up to hit the ball, found myself short of breath and fell in front of their feet convulsing gasping for air. I thought I was having a heart attack and they called 911. Fortunately it was a false alarm but after the doctors did blood test on me they were able to find out that it was one of those accidents that was a message from God. Then they did my blood test and here's what they found. Yada yada yada yada. Okay, what else is attached to it? A condom? This is actually really too easy with a blood test. I mean that, come on. You know, come on. You know, I mean if I would have had this little inexpensive piece of rubber I wouldn't have that 23 year old kid that had cost me $400,000. No, that one, I'm not going to touch. Off a band-aid. Yeah, that's a good one. Dear friend, as you can see I have attached a band-aid to a flesh colored, a small flesh colored band-aid at the top of this letter. Why have I done this? The answer is easy. I personally have always been afraid of needles but recently a friend convinced me that there was a test I could take that is so important that I should overcome my fear of, you know, these procedures. Well as it turned out I didn't even feel the needle going in. I said when are you going to do it? And they said it's already over. As a matter of fact, if you'll look, we've already put the band-aid on. You know, and I was amazed at that. This thing was painless but not only was it painless, it has changed my life because let me tell you what they did. They took some blood and they yada yada yada yada. Do a couple more steps. Tweezers. Dear friend, as you can see I have attached a pair of tweezers to the top of this letter. Why have I done this? The answer is easy. My name is Gene Dovele and for the past 10 years I've had a great deal of trouble masturbating because I haven't... Oh! I'm sorry. I'm sorry. I really, I'm sorry. I'm sorry. To the viewing audience, I don't want to be sued. That's not true. I mean, actually, well I don't know if it's true. Let me say that. Your luck might be... Yeah, he... Yeah, he does. But anyway, a pair of tweezers... Now you've got me off the track there, so I guess I'll have to come up with something for tweezers. Uh-oh. Don't do that. Dear friend, as you can see I have attached a pair of tweezers to the top of this letter. Why have I done this? The answer is easy. If you have ever watched medical technicians at work, you will see that often in front of them on an examining table, they will have bits, they will have little microscopic bits of tissue samples, cell samples, blood platelets and so forth, that they have to pick up with tweezers in place between the slides that they put under a microscope. Recently, they used a pair of tweezers to take part of a tissue sample or a blood sample, pick up a slide of my blood to put under a microscope, and it changed my life so much that forevermore, every time I see a pair of tweezers, I think about this. Here's what happens. Yada yada yada. Do you understand what I'm talking about now with segues? Okay, now, let's get into the formula of the letter. And I want to review the first things, the things that we've already gone on, because I would rather have hammer home the fundamentals than teach you how to do Benchly's fifth quantum thesis on parabolic manifestations when you do regression analysis on mailing lists. First thing you want to do is you want to become familiar with pools of hungry fish. By reading the SRDS, you want to find fish that are hungry for what you are selling, that have demonstrated that they are real hungry because they will pay a lot of money, that they have done this over and over, and that they have done it recently. You want to put the odds on your side to market these groups of people. If you go wrong, this is the number one reason that you will go wrong. If you don't have something else to sell to these people, if you don't have a product, make it an information-based product. Sell this by direct mail if you are just starting out in the direct response business and do it with sales letter. The sales letter, we've already talked a lot about it. The thing that you do to create a sales letter is you go through the Masterpiece Collection of letters I've given you, and in your own handwriting or on your own word processor, you handwrite or type out every headline in there. You handwrite or type out every first sentence in there, and you handwrite or type out every close that is in there, every call to action. The next thing you do is you arrange to get yourself on the telephone talking to someone who is a logical prospect of what you are selling, and just conversationally explain to them what it is, all the details about it, the benefits of it, the facts about it, why they should buy it, and so forth. You do this over and over until you have captured yourself at concert pitch on tape, and you have that tape transcribed. You have Xerox coffins made of that transcription. You go over it and with a yellow highlighter pen, the first thing you do is you highlight all the benefits of your product or service, and then you write each one on a 3x5 index card. The second thing you do is you go through the second copy of the transcript you highlighted, and you highlight each fact about your product or service, and you write that fact on a 3x5 index card. Then you have all those facts sitting before you, and you make a third group. These are still facts, but these facts come into the area of proof slash validation, reasons why someone can't believe you. Then what you do is you find the best template letter or the best series of template letters that you could find that could be used for what you are selling, and you write out those template letters in your own handwriting two or three times, and then you take a break and try to put this project out of your mind. Then you go back, have been written these, and then you start reading the collection of headlines that you will have. You begin, well first you isolate from all this work the number one thing, the number one thing or benefit that comes from what you have. You keep that in mind, and you go through the headline exercise that we did yesterday until one of them just leaks out of your mind that fits there. Then you will have your headline, and then you will have written a bunch of first sentences. Now what we just talked about were segways, and now we're going to go into something I also believe is supremely important. The number one reason that people do not buy from you is they don't want what you're selling. That's it, and that's the thing that everybody wastes all their energy trying to overcome. I have these neon raincoats that they turn purple when it rains or pink when it turns rain. People don't want to buy them. They keep trying to explain to them why they should buy them. Forget that, you're not in the education business. People do want what you have to sell, and they are likely to want it more than the money that it would cost them to get it, and they still don't buy it. What's the reason that they don't buy it? Exactly, they do not believe you. Now, you're getting a letter from a stranger. Let's start making a list of all the reasons why he could think of that he could make you believe him. We'll just start right here. What's the reason that you would believe a letter from a stranger? What could he put in it? We're going to go clear around a testimony. Write down a testimonial. There's a guy who invented a product called Kube-Ray, and it really works well. It comes in a tube, and it darkens your scalp to the same color as your hair. And Blake Thomas wrote the ad for it about 12 years ago, and they couldn't come up with the headline. And I came up with the headline, You Losing Your Hair, Here's How To Keep It A Secret. And that's so effective. That ad is still running today, 12 years later. It's exactly worth it. Um, in the old days. You look like a kind of a bandit. Thank you. You're welcome. Well, you could if you got permission to close the copy of the favorable state of the magazine. This was only half the report. The other idea I have is when you use testimonials, you can get permission to close the first and give the testimonials phone number so people can call them and check. Those are two jackpot observations. And here's what you do a lot of times. You write the article that appears in the paper and then you run it in a paper where it's inexpensive to run. And the Key West citizen is very inexpensive to run a full page. Now, they don't have a big circulation and don't expect to make a profit from your ad. That's not the reason for running there. The reason is they will run off reprints for you for real inexpensive like 20 bucks a thousand and a full page and print it front and back. Or you can run a smaller part of the paper and you can run it in investors business daily, which has a lot of prestige and is relatively low cost. Depending on what you're selling, you can run in spotlight pretty inexpensively, which is a weekly newspaper that is mailed to paranoid right-wing radicals. The point is, is you can make this appear and you can say, and here's what people read about me in the New York Times on April 17, 1994. And it will be true. That's a great way to validate things. What was the other one? I thought of giving, well I'll just give up some more of it. But let's go to the one that I... Giving out the action with phone numbers. Yes, I wanted to enlarge on that. There's something that I did on the most successful diet at Everrith. It came from a doctor and we invited the people to call him. And we had his, you know, an answering machine on there. And he actually took the call sometimes, I believe. But the fact of the matter is it's not that they would call, it's that they could call and get him. So add these lines to your copy whenever appropriate. If you have any questions, you can call me at, and then put your real... put a POTS number there. POTS and POTS, Plano Telephone Service. You want to give people the idea, and actually somewhat you want it to be reality, that they can reach out and get in touch with you. And the other... Okay, say it's 12 bucks a month, and you could have 10 telephone numbers in your ad, and you buy 10 answering machines and 10 lines, and it really credentials. That is a very good one. The next one I have is give them some kind of information-based product. Give them a free sample of your information and either a special report, issue of a news letter, or a pair of analytics, or an ad character. He said, give them a sample of what you can do. You know, when people subscribe to my newsletter, they get a welcome package with the letter, with the dollar bill letter, and they immediately get seven back issues free in a sealed back envelope. And, you know, you read it, and then you know what you're getting. So in many cases, giving them a sample. Young man? Yeah, I just wanted to pre-trial. Pre-trial is great. If you can do it, in fact, and you can do it, you should always try, I won't cash your check or process your credit card order for a period of 31 days. It will always pencil out to be the most profitable thing to do. I defy you ever to find once when it does not. You know, unless, of course, you're selling something garbage. You know, if you're selling garbage, then you shouldn't be selling it anyway. Okay, pervert, you're up. Well, what I do is I offer half-bite orders to turn it up to favorite clients. I have them offer half-down, they have them for 25 days. That's not the first mailings. And then as I go into sequential mailings, I start breaking it up into different payments. And I go up to can of four, 700 bucks to break it up into six monthly installments. You know who did that better than anybody was Charles Atlas. Remember those ads about the guy kicking sand in your face? And he'd send you the offer for his dynamic tension course, and then he'd offer it to you at payments just like that, and then he'd offer you a damaged set. So what he's saying categorizes it this way, lesser and lesser initial risk. And whether you made a good point, there's a couple of people who can actually understand. The dynamic tension is the way of repackaging something, you know, what dynamic tension is moving your arm like this. But he called it dynamic tension. That is the term of calling something something. And that is, if you think about that, you know, the guy built his business up calling something dynamic tension. It sounds really fancy. It does. It built a great business. There's another element to what he said, that when you help offer a payment plan, it goes to establish a user that's going to be there. That's a good plan. That's a good thinking. It does. When you give a payment plan, it establishes the idea that you are going to be there. You have a patent for a copy of the front page. Excellent. Excellent. You notice we did that yesterday at the golf club? It's protected by patents, and we put it there. By the way, if you have a really good invention, patenting it is the stupidest thing you can do. Here's what happens in reality when you patent an invention. If it's a really good invention, a big company will rip off the patent, you will sue them, and they will be glad to litigate with you until you are dead, you're broke, and they're rich. You have way too much hair, and it looks too good. I don't like that. Leonardo? With the testimonials, not only to include the full name, but also the professional person and the age of the person. Good point. John Q. Everett, attorney, age 47, Maslin, Ohio, 216, 229, 10, whatever it is, you know? I found out that testimonials, I had in my eyes, lots of people were actually from the community, and they are of course the most... best... Yeah, you could really find this people on the telephone. Yeah. People will call testimonials, but really only a tiny fraction of people will call. The more important thing is that everybody who reads your ad knows that they could call. And even if they do call and get a recorded message and say, hi, this is John Everett, I've been getting a lot of calls like this, and it's hard for me to answer them all, but if you'd like to leave a message for me, you can do so with tone, or if you'd like to write me or blah, blah, blah. You know, and there was somebody there. Old South in the mouth. Double money back guarantee? Yeah, that's a really hot one. Star that one. Double money back guarantee. Now, the way you should feel about guarantees, I will repeat, is that you should be willing to make a guarantee, any guarantee that is legit. And sometimes you will want to say in your copy, if you are dissatisfied for any reason, parentheses, or no reason at all, close parentheses, all you have to do is return the product within 60 days, and you will get an immediate and 100% complete refund with no questions asked. In fact, if you use this product as instructed, and it does not produce the results that we promise in this letter, not only will we refund your money, we will give you double the money back. Now, what you do is you make that a conditional guarantee. I can guarantee that people who use the advertising methods that I espouse in my newsletter and seminars, I can guarantee that they will work. I can't guarantee that every time, but they will work. But there is one situation in which what I teach will never work. That's if you don't do it. And I do not accept anybody's babble anymore when they say, why didn't you do it? I don't know, it just didn't seem like it would work for me. And it's like someone sold me a membership to a gym in Santa Monica, in Westwood, California, right near OJ's house this morning. I'm already going to take another cut at marriage. But anyway, I always take a stab at it in another stab at marriage. You want to know the most terrifying thing I ever experienced, I ever had, my friend Don Kamp told me about it. He was at a convention in Las Vegas with a bunch of attorneys, and he ended up on an airplane. He was the only person who was not an attorney except for the pilot. The plane was hijacked, and they took it to a distant city, and the hijackers made a chilling threat. They said that we want $10 million in cash, and that the plane would be able to fly any place we want, but we're going to start releasing the attorneys one every hour. Okay, hit. I guess my hair is going to make me feel happy. Yeah, give me an idea of the problem I'm going to face this week. Actually, you think, oh my goodness, I got one that's slammed up for me. If you have a title and you're a doctor, you do have credentials, but use it and portray it whenever you do. I always put down dunker and paint-k-weathers, no matter when I write to Dennis or not. And if I'm writing to my colleagues, and I put down all the credentials from members of such and such organizations, the number of years I've been in practice and all of that sort of thing, all like CV information. It doesn't help me with that. Not the information, CV information, initials after your appointment. This is a man who can't believe anything. Who really wants to be short. But you know what? There is nothing more insufferable than someone who has not experienced bankruptcy, the loss of a business, he has not experienced bankruptcy, the loss of a business, his woman or leaving him or her man leaving him and everything. I mean, these people think this is the way it's going to be forever and they have an ego and an outlook on life that's absolutely insufferable. I love, I like my people who have been crawled through the muck and shelled up a shot with a machine gun and survived it all and still be smiling. That's the people I like to hang out with. The other one is basically to hit them with more than one media. Okay, that's an interesting point. Hit them with more than one media. Years ago, I invented an advertising breakthrough called RUB. That's an acronym for Radio Umbrella of Bullshit. What we did is we found that when we ran a full-page ad in the newspaper and backed it up by advertising the advertising on radio. Basically, it would say look for this ad that we tripled the results. The formula I used was that for every thousand circulation of the paper, I would spend $3 on radio advertising the advertising. And when you go into a radio go into a talk, a station that has a talk show on it or a station that has country music on it. And the reason for that is people listen to those stations. Many of the other stations, they just have going on in the background kind of like an electronic fireplace. Statistics show that the average American watches television 8, 9, 10 hours a day or whatever that is. That's really not true. They have it on. I have my TV on a lot. And with the sound completely off because it's like an electronic fireplace. I am very particular about the shows I put on TV that I don't watch. And I'll tell you this. That is an excellent way to credential something. We're in the service business. We serve as clients and air-conditioners. We come up with a bunch of this phenomenon that we're using in the service business. It's better than a money-back guarantee. We say to them, if you don't think they were the best service you've ever had, you don't have to pay for the repair to begin with. Okay? I want to comment on that because that's an important observation. Everything else being equal, the strongest guarantee wins. You all know this story or some of you probably don't. So it's worth repeating. The story about the two farmers who both had a mule to sell and one of them talked to the prospective buyer and told him all the virtues of the mule and how much it cost, etc., etc. The second farmer did the same thing but said, take the mule and use it for a month. If you like her, keep her. If you don't bring it back, you'll have to pay for it. The second farmer obviously got it safe. Now, there's limitations on how much you can do that in practicality. But I'll tell you how they used to sell color televisions when they first came out. It's really neat. I got to remember they're new. A guy would come into the television store and there would be this new expensive color TV and he'd be there with his wife and he'd ask the salesman how much is this and yada yada salesman and say, look, these things are new and he'd ask her for everybody. He said, I don't recommend you necessarily buy this thing. He said, look, let me do this. Take it home. Don't pay anything for it. Use it for a week or so and then make up your mind and we'll talk about it. Then they would take the television home and then the salesman would come out and expecting the guy to hear a pitch. He wouldn't. He said, I just came out to check on it. See if the color is right and then need to make any little adjustments, etc. Do you think that his wife and children were going to let him take that TV back after having it for a while? Color TV is normal today. It's pretty commonplace. But what do you think the chances of that salesman making a sale by doing that? Almost 100%. That's the way cars used to be sold. To remember when you could tell a dodge from a Hyundai and when I was a kid we knew the make your model of every car and the day it came out ask anybody told enough. It was a national event. Tomorrow is the day the new Ford's come out. You know how they sold the new cars? A guy would come in on Friday and he would say, hey look just take it home over the weekend. I know you. Drive around and see how you feel about coming back and talking. Guess what the guy does? He goes home, picks up mama, the kid drives around, picks up his breastprint, you know shows off the car, etc. And on Monday, do you think he wants to face somebody and says, where's that great new Ford can have? Uh-uh. Let's him do it. So that's a good one. Sir. The only thing I think I've left, I'll talk about a lot of photos but I don't think anybody said a photo of the person writing the letter. That's a good one though. And as I pointed out yesterday even if you did nothing except include a photograph of yourself. Say hi, my name is John J. McGillicuddy. I don't think we know each other but I'd like, you know I kind of like people to get an idea but just put your photo in there. Just do something. Just like a photo. Just like a photo. Yes, an act. Well, you can print a photo on the letter but a free standing photo is more compelling. Now, do you all know how to make fake photos that look like real ones? You do not? This is a technique that brought in untold millions of dollars for the Code of Arms Company. Here's how you do it. You take a photograph of like yourself or whatever it is and you get 150 of those photos developed. You take it to a printer and you have him pasted up on a big layout sheet with white space between each part of it. And then what he does is he this is about right, this is a genius. Right in principle? Now, is about the same principle. And so he has this thing that's the size of his print plate and they take a photograph of it and they scan it through a computer and then they print out one sheet if they make the color separation and they print out one sheet of paper with 150 or 200 photographs on it. There's white edges around and you slice them and dice them and then there are individual photos and then the way I do it on the back in blue ink it says act me photo, act me photo, act me photo and it looks like a real photograph. Okay? We're going to take a break right after I tell you how not to advertise the advertising. I've told this story at a number of times but I think it makes a point that is extremely important. Humor is very helpful in creating an educational experience. It is very seldom helpful when you're trying to create a sale especially if you're trying to sell a string. I suggest that you just do not attempt it at all. I'm going to tell this. I think I'm a pretty clever guy and if there's anything that you should succeed at selling it's a weight loss product. And I wrote a full page ad to sell a weight loss book. I think it was a weight loss book and ran it into the Akron Beacon Journal and I think at that time it had 250,000 readers and I only got three orders for my weight loss product which was low price in spite of the fact that I advertised my advertising on the radio in Akron, Ohio. It's a little difficult to understand why I didn't get more orders but I still remember that radio ad and I'm proud of it that I did it myself. And for four days before the ad appeared here's what the people of Akron, Ohio heard from Gary Halberts voice. Attention all fat people. Does your garbage man deliver instead of collect and then say chow time? Whenever you get on an elevator no matter what button you push doesn't always go down. When you go shopping at the supermarket your neighbors laugh behind you back and say that little kid you went to market again. That ain't where it's at. Learn how to get rid of it. Read the Akron Beacon Journal. Look for the full page ad this Thursday that says the world has too many fat people in it. Here's how you can help get rid of them. And then I said buy quirky. The thrust of the ad was that fat people placed an unfair burden on the rest of humanity. It takes too much cloth to clothe them. It takes more gas to haul their fat asses around. It takes more energy to keep them warm, etc. etc. That was not appreciated. The campaign did not work. It was a dismal failure. And I will tell you something. I make a lot of fun of porters and I have always felt that I hold nothing sacred. I'll make fun of anybody. But I never felt too bad about that because I have been a great deal of my life of porcarum myself. And I figured I hated it so much the only thing I could do was laugh at it. You may notice I'm a little thinner now. Actually something happened to me that was very very inspiring last year. And as a result of that I determined that I was going to live my life optimally. And I was going to achieve the best physical health that I could and the best life I had. It was something really inspirational happening. I'll tell you about that. Something you may be interested to know especially any of you who are interested in losing weight and losing and achieving health. I was under water at the University of Miami and that's the real way to tell how much of your weight is body fat and how much is muscle. And I had another body fat test about two and a half weeks ago and I've lost 55 pounds of fat and gained 22 pounds of muscle. My waist has gone from something like 42 to 44 to where I can almost wear size 32 jeans. I've been stronger than I've ever been in my life. I can leg press 600 pounds and people don't kick sand in my face as much as they used to. I am now routinely rejected by women as I was in the past but as I said it's a higher quality of women who reject me now. And if any of you are interested in the real way to do this this is not the subject of the seminar. I can tell you because I made a point to find out what really does work. Living in South Beach if you all looked around there one of the problems I was having going about my normal life is that if you're more than 15 pounds overweight you have to have a special permit to go outside. That's not true. But you notice that if you could be there today during the daytime I mean it was getting embarrassing for me to go outside. They got guys so good looking they turned my head I go well you know that can't even be real. No, forget it. That's what happened between us was just once. It's never going to happen again.
